methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $14M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Aclarity is a Series A climate and energy company founded by Julie Bliss Mullen that has raised $14M to date. Valuation analysis draws on a comparison set of 3 same-stage, same-sector private peers (Graphyte, Fourth Power, Steady Energy) with valuations ranging from $110M to $200M, alongside 1 public comparable and a broader universe of 8 frontier-tech energy companies.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression, anchoring to the $14M total raised as a proxy for entry value and applying a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A to next-stage advancement, implies a $28M valuation. Peer-median valuation across the 3-company comparison set yields $150M, with a range of $110M–$200M. The stark divergence between methods reflects the wide dispersion in frontier-energy valuations at this stage and uncertainty around Aclarity's relative operational maturity and market traction versus named peers.

Triangulation of these two methods produces a low-end estimate of $24M, a median of $89M, and a high-end estimate of $172M. The range widens substantially because stage-progression relies on cost-of-capital assumptions while peer-median captures actual market pricing with high variance. Key uncertainties include Aclarity's current revenue or unit economics relative to peers, the relevance of public comparables to early-stage private valuations, and whether the 3-peer set adequately reflects market sentiment for the company's specific technology and go-to-market position.
